[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2023-11-21
[TITLE]: Fort Drum soldiers treated to early Thanksgiving feasts
[METADATA/INTRO]: Fort Drum soldiers treated to early Thanksgiving feasts FORT DRUM, New York (WWNY) - Members of the 10th Mountain Division celebrated an early Thanksgiving with a series of feasts across Fort Drum on Tuesday....
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: “Things like the cornucopia, the displays, the unit cohesion. Things of that nature. It’s a very competitive environment to see and observe,” said Mills. 
 “I think we have the chance, the opportunity to win so I’m very much excited about that,” said Donegan. 
 The 1st Brigade’s ace in the whole was an entire roasted pig, a culinary experience unique to its dining facility. 
 “We try to make this our signature because a lot of other dining facilities don’t do it. We try to make this our own because we do it the best,” said Mills. 
 Above all else the meal is the military’s way of giving an authentic Thanksgiving experience to soldiers who are a long way from home, like Specialist David House from California.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: Unknown Date
[TITLE]: Thanksgiving meal warms the hearts of soldiers at Fort Jackson | wltx.com
[METADATA/INTRO]: Thanksgiving arrived early at Fort Jackson. Nearly 1,200 soldiers at Fort Jackson descended upon a dining facility on the base for a pre-Thanksgiving meal on Wednesday....
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Thanksgiving arrived early at Fort Jackson. 
 Nearly 1,200 soldiers at Fort Jackson descended upon a dining facility on the base for a pre-Thanksgiving meal on Wednesday. 
 Following tradition, the meal is served by commanders, their staff and senior non-commissioned officers of each company as their soldiers pass through the line. 
 "Serving us was a bit of a shock," says Savionne Baylock, soldier trainee. "But it’s nice to see that they’re taking initiative and feeding us on this Thanksgiving. Today is just one of those days you can dig in and fill your belly up and not have to worry about it."

[PUBLISHED DATE]: Unknown Date
[TITLE]: Fort Jackson soldiers-in-training served early Thanksgiving Meal | wltx.com
[METADATA/INTRO]: Columbia, SC (WLTX) -- Thanksgiving came a little early for soldiers-in-training at Fort Jackson. For many, it's their first holiday away from home. Every year, Army commanders at Fort Jackson serve a Thanksgiving meal to trainees....
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Columbia, SC (WLTX) -- Thanksgiving came a little early for soldiers-in-training at Fort Jackson. For many, it's their first holiday away from home. 
 Every year, Army commanders at Fort Jackson serve a Thanksgiving meal to trainees. 
 "This is what we call the Super Bowl," said Kenneth Harrison, General Manager of Building 11500 at Fort Jackson. "It's just something with a 'Wow'! A 'wow' factor, in other words. [Soldiers] see something different. They see people out of their uniform, they see people looking like civilians." 
 "They get served by their leaders, by their drill sergeants," said Brigadier General Milford Beagle, Jr. "They get to see all of us in here catering to them. That is what servant leadership is all about."
